paper_title: Anti-tumor efficacy of a potent and selective non-covalent KRASG12D inhibitor
hypothesis: MRTX1133 is a potent, selective, non-covalent inhibitor of KRASG12D that demonstrates anti-tumor efficacy through direct binding to both GDP and GTP-bound states

stages.description: Predict KRASG12D structure and validate against known crystal structures

jobs.description: Predict structure of KRASG12D to validate mutant conformation
validates_claims: KRASG12D has specific structural features that enable MRTX1133 binding

stages.description: Validate MRTX1133 binding to KRASG12D switch II pocket using multiple docking approaches

jobs.description: Test MRTX1133 binding to KRASG12D using DiffDock
validates_claims: MRTX1133 binds to the switch II pocket of KRASG12D
MRTX1133 demonstrates high-affinity binding with KD ~0.2 pM

claims.claim: MRTX1133 binds to the switch II pocket of KRASG12D
MRTX1133 demonstrates high-affinity binding with KD ~0.2 pM
MRTX1133 has ~700-fold selectivity for KRASG12D over KRASWT
MRTX1133 binds to both GDP and GTP-bound states of KRASG12D
MRTX1133 binding induces conformational changes in switch I and II regions
MRTX1133 binding eliminates KRASG12D surface competency for effector binding
KRASG12D has specific structural features that enable MRTX1133 binding
MRTX1133 demonstrates tumor regression in KRASG12D-mutant models
